title: lidbeckia turbinata
intro: Lidbeckia turbinata, also known as the Wild Marigold, is a flowering plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to South Africa. Traditionally, Lidbeckia turbinata has been used in herbal medicine, attributed with various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant's aerial parts are often used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote relief from various health issues. Although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy, Lidbeckia turbinata has been traditionally used to support immune function and provide relief from pain and inflammation.
